channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Karnataka High Court grants bail to West Bengal woman with minor children after demolition drive incident
item.description: The Karnataka High Court has granted bail to Sarbanu Khatun, a 25-year-old woman from West Bengal, who was arrested in January during a demolition drive in Bengaluru for shouting the slogan 'Jai Bangl...

item.title: 18-day-old baby dies after falling from third-floor flat in London; mother charged with murder
item.description: An 18-day-old infant, Baby Mariam, tragically died after allegedly being thrown from a third-floor flat in Westminster, central London. The incident reportedly occurred while her father was in the kit...

item.title: Pakistan maintains airspace closure for Indian aircraft until April 24, 2026
item.description: Pakistan has decided to continue its restriction on Indian airlines and India-registered aircraft from using its airspace, with the ban now extended until April 24, 2026. The Pakistan Airports Authori...

item.title: Imran Khan's health assessed by specialist medical board at Adiala jail
item.description: The health of former Prime Minister Imran Khan has been thoroughly assessed by a newly formed medical board at Adiala Jail, Islamabad, following directions from the Islamabad High Court (IHC). The boa...

item.title: Moderate Coffee and Tea Intake Linked to Lower Dementia Risk Over 43 Years
item.description: A long-term study conducted by Mass General Brigham, Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health, and the Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard suggests that moderate consumption of caffeinated coffee and ...

item.title: Study finds couples often disturb each other's sleep, suggesting separate bedrooms can improve rest
item.description: A recent study has revealed that couples who share a bed disturb each other's sleep on average six times per night. These disturbances, caused by movements such as rolling over, tugging at covers, or ...

item.title: Sean Penn receives symbolic award from Ukrainian railway carriage
item.description: Actor Sean Penn, 65, was presented with a symbolic 'Oscar' made from the metal of a Ukrainian railway carriage destroyed by a Russian missile. This gesture followed his absence from the official Acade...

item.title: Treasurer Signals Budget Savings Amid Rising Inflation and Petrol Costs
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers has indicated that the upcoming May budget will include significant savings measures as the government grapples with rising inflation. New Treasury modelling suggests inflation ...

item.title: Tanzania Sees Smaller Current Account Deficit Thanks To Strong Exports And Tourism Recovery
item.description: Dar es Salaam — Tanzania's current account deficit has improved over the past year, thanks to better performance in exports and a recovery in tourism. According to the Bank of Tanzania’s latest Monthl...

item.title: CRDB Bank Foundation receives 3,967 applications from youth for business support
item.description: Dar es Salaam: CRDB Bank Foundation, through its Director Tuliesther Mwambapa, is encouraging Tanzanian youth to actively grab opportunities for economic independence. During a ceremony in Dar es Sala...

item.title: Philippine Coast Guard deployed to Panatag to protect fishermen amid Chinese activity
item.description: The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) has dispatched both ships and an aircraft to Bajo de Masinloc, locally known as Panatag Shoal, after Filipino fishermen reported being harassed by Chinese vessels in t...

item.title: Australia’s fuel supply is stable despite public concerns
item.description: Despite media reports suggesting a looming fuel crisis in Australia, the reality is far less dramatic. Concerns arose after the government announced the nation had only 30 days of fuel reserves, promp...

item.title: CBN Governor says Nigeria’s economy resilient amid global economic challenges
item.description: The Governor of the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN), Olayemi Cardoso, don talk say Naija economy don dey strong and ready to handle wahala from global economic shocks. As he dey speak for Africa Capital...

item.title: AIPAC Experiences Mixed Outcomes in Illinois Democratic Primaries, Prompting Internal and External Criticism
item.description: The American Israel Public Affairs Committee (AIPAC) encountered mixed results in its first significant test during the 2026 midterm primaries in Illinois, where it invested heavily in four competitiv...

item.title: Gondola Detaches at Swiss Ski Resort, One Person Seriously Injured
item.description: A serious accident occurred on Wednesday at a ski resort in the canton of Obwalden, Switzerland, when a gondola detached from its cable and rolled down a snow-covered mountainside. Dramatic footage ca...

item.title: Funding cuts threaten closure of Niagara Region’s safe drug consumption site, raising public health concerns
item.description: The Niagara Region’s only safe drug consumption site in St. Catharines, Ontario, is facing closure after the provincial government announced that funding for the site will end in 90 days. Positive Liv...

item.title: Nagpur Gangster Arrested With Help of Partner and Lawyer
item.description: In a significant development from Nagpur, the police successfully apprehended a notorious gangster with the assistance of his live-in partner and a lawyer. According to reports, the gangster had been ...

item.title: NSC DG Olopade Supports Senator Adeola, Calls on Ogun Youths to Engage in Politics
item.description: For Ogun State, politics dey hot as the Director-General of the National Sports Commission, Bukola Olopade, don openly support Senator Olamilekan Adeola for the 2027 governorship. Olopade talk say Ade...

item.title: 2026 Assembly Polls Updates: Key Developments Including Bordoloi's Switch to BJP, DMK-CPI Alliance, Observer Deployments, and MCC Enforcement
item.description: On March 18, 2026, several significant updates emerged regarding the upcoming Assembly elections in Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, West Bengal, and Puducherry. Assam MP Pradyut Bordoloi resigned from Cong...

item.title: Zambia debates Tobacco Control Bill amid economic and health concerns
item.description: Zambia is currently embroiled in a heated debate over a proposed Tobacco Control Bill, which aims to protect citizens from the severe health, social, and economic consequences of tobacco use. The Bill...
